model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: St. John's enters as an 11-11.5 point favorite and holds a commanding ~13-point AdjEM advantage over Northern Iowa (KenPom #20 vs. #71, Torvik #18 vs. #70), with the Red Storm's elite defense ranked 12th nationally posing a severe matchup problem for a Panthers offense that ranks 151st in adjusted efficiency. Northern Iowa won the MVC Tournament as a non-favorite with a modest 23-12 overall record and is making its first NCAA Tournament appearance since 2016, limiting their tournament-experience edge. Rick Pitino's Red Storm are battle-tested from a grueling Big East schedule and showed dominant late-season form with a 72-52 win over UConn, and Jay Bilas specifically cited Pitino's first-round coaching track record in picking St. John's. While 12-over-5 upsets occur historically at a meaningful rate, Northern Iowa's offensive limitations make this one of the least credible upset scenarios in the bracket.

reasoningStep.title: St. John's vs. Northern Iowa — Round of 64 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: St. John's holds a dominant efficiency edge, elite defense ranked 12th nationally, and a large betting line advantage. Northern Iowa's weak offense (ranked ~151st) is a critical liability against the Red Storm's defense, and the Panthers' 23-12 record and non-favorite MVC Tournament path undercut their upset credentials. Expert consensus and the betting market both strongly favor St. John's.
evidence: KenPom: St. John's #20 (+24.8 AdjEM) vs. Northern Iowa #71 (+11.86 AdjEM) — a ~13-point efficiency gap
Torvik: St. John's #18 (AdjDE 94.8, rank #12 nationally) vs. Northern Iowa #70 (AdjOE 109.7, rank #151 nationally)
Betting market: St. John's opened as an 11-11.5 point favorite, one of the largest spreads in any 5/12 matchup
Northern Iowa profile: 23-12 overall, won MVC Tournament as a non-favorite, first NCAA Tournament since 2016
Jay Bilas (ESPN) picked St. John's, citing Rick Pitino's strong first-round coaching record and the Red Storm's overall dominance
St. John's recent form: beat UConn 72-52 in Big East Tournament, demonstrating elite late-season performance

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Get To Know The 2026 MVC Tournament Champs: Northern Iowa Basketball Skip to content CBB Review (College Basketball Review) College Basketball Power Rankings, Interviews and more! ACC Boston College Cal Clemson Duke Florida State Georgia Tech Louisville Miami (Fla) NC State North Carolina Notre Dame Pitt SMU Stanford Syracuse Virginia Virginia Tech Wake Forest Big East Butler Creighton DePaul Georgetown Marquette Providence Seton Hall St. John’s UConn Villanova Xavier Big Ten Illinois Indiana Iowa Maryland Michigan Michigan State Minnesota Nebraska Northwestern Ohio State Oregon Penn State Purdue Rutgers UCLA USC Washington Wisconsin Big 12 Arizona Arizona State Baylor BYU Cincinnati Colorado Houston Iowa State Kansas Kansas State Oklahoma State TCU Texas Tech UCF Utah West Virginia SEC Alabama Arkansas Auburn Florida Georgia Kentucky LSU Mississippi State Missouri Oklahoma Ole Miss South Carolina Tennessee Texas Texas A&M Vanderbilt Mid Majors AAC Atlantic Sun Atlantic 10 C-USA Horizon MAAC MAC Missouri Valley Mountain West SoCon WAC WCC Oregon State Washington State Bracketology Matty Brackets March on CBB Review Rankings Meet Our Staff Get To Know The 2026 MVC Tournament Champs: Northern Iowa Basketball By Mat Mlodzinski Mar 16, 2026 Northern Iowa basketball logo Northern Iowa basketball will play a St. John’s team on fire in the always fun 5-12 seed game. But can the Panthers find enough offense to make it a game? The 12 seed over the 5 seed is always one of the most popular upset picks, with one happening in each of the last two seasons, and consistently in most NCAA Tournaments. This Northern Iowa basketball team isn’t your typical 12 seed. They certainly weren’t one of the favorites to win the always tough MVC Tournament , but they got hot at the right time to win Arch Madness, and now will take on St. John’s all the way in San Diego, CA on Friday. How they got there: As I said before, UNI entered the MVC Tournament outside of the projected winners, as the 6 seed in Arch Madness. But they continued their defensive effort, holding Evansville to 59 points in round one and Illinois State to 52 in the quarterfinals. From there, the Panthers’ offense started heating up, taking down 2-seed Bradley 73-69 in the semis, and not playing with their food in the championship, with an 84-69 win over UIC. It now marks the ninth NCAA Tournament appearance for the Panthers. And they’ve won a few times, most famously with Ali Farokmanesh guiding them to the Sweet 16 in 2010. 1990: 14 seed, knocked off 3 seed Missouri in first round 2010: 9 seed, knocked off 8 seed UNLV in first round and #1 seed Kansas in second round 2015: 5 seed, knocked off 12 seed Wyoming in first round 2016: 11 seed, knocked off 6 seed Texas in first round This is their first trip back to March Madness in a decade, but it’s important to let you know that the last three times UNI made it, they won at least a game, and Ben Jacobson’s

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

result.text: Get To Know The 2026 MVC Tournament Champs: Northern Iowa Basketball Skip to content CBB Review (College Basketball Review) College Basketball Power Rankings, Interviews and more! ACC Boston College Cal Clemson Duke Florida State Georgia Tech Louisville Miami (Fla) NC State North Carolina Notre Dame Pitt SMU Stanford Syracuse Virginia Virginia Tech Wake Forest Big East Butler Creighton DePaul Georgetown Marquette Providence Seton Hall St. John’s UConn Villanova Xavier Big Ten Illinois Indiana Iowa Maryland Michigan Michigan State Minnesota Nebraska Northwestern Ohio State Oregon Penn State Purdue Rutgers UCLA USC Washington Wisconsin Big 12 Arizona Arizona State Baylor BYU Cincinnati Colorado Houston Iowa State Kansas Kansas State Oklahoma State TCU Texas Tech UCF Utah West Virginia SEC Alabama Arkansas Auburn Florida Georgia Kentucky LSU Mississippi State Missouri Oklahoma Ole Miss South Carolina Tennessee Texas Texas A&M Vanderbilt Mid Majors AAC Atlantic Sun Atlantic 10 C-USA Horizon MAAC MAC Missouri Valley Mountain West SoCon WAC WCC Oregon State Washington State Bracketology Matty Brackets March on CBB Review Rankings Meet Our Staff Get To Know The 2026 MVC Tournament Champs: Northern Iowa Basketball By Mat Mlodzinski Mar 16, 2026 Northern Iowa basketball logo Northern Iowa basketball will play a St. John’s team on fire in the always fun 5-12 seed game. But can the Panthers find enough offense to make it a game? The 12 seed over the 5 seed is always one of the most popular upset picks, with one happening in each of the last two seasons, and consistently in most NCAA Tournaments. This Northern Iowa basketball team isn’t your typical 12 seed. They certainly weren’t one of the favorites to win the always tough MVC Tournament , but they got hot at the right time to win Arch Madness, and now will take on St. John’s all the way in San Diego, CA on Friday. How they got there: As I said before, UNI entered the MVC Tournament outside of the projected winners, as the 6 seed in Arch Madness. But they continued their defensive effort, holding Evansville to 59 points in round one and Illinois State to 52 in the quarterfinals. From there, the Panthers’ offense started heating up, taking down 2-seed Bradley 73-69 in the semis, and not playing with their food in the championship, with an 84-69 win over UIC. It now marks the ninth NCAA Tournament appearance for the Panthers. And they’ve won a few times, most famously with Ali Farokmanesh guiding them to the Sweet 16 in 2010. 1990: 14 seed, knocked off 3 seed Missouri in first round 2010: 9 seed, knocked off 8 seed UNLV in first round and #1 seed Kansas in second round 2015: 5 seed, knocked off 12 seed Wyoming in first round 2016: 11 seed, knocked off 6 seed Texas in first round This is their first trip back to March Madness in a decade, but it’s important to let you know that the last three times UNI made it, they won at least a game, and Ben Jacobson’s

modelTrace.content: {"pick":{"gameId":"r64-east-3","winnerId":"st-john-s","confidence":80,"rationale":"St. John's enters as an 11-11.5 point favorite and holds a commanding ~13-point AdjEM advantage over Northern Iowa (KenPom #20 vs. #71, Torvik #18 vs. #70), with the Red Storm's elite defense ranked 12th nationally posing a severe matchup problem for a Panthers offense that ranks 151st in adjusted efficiency. Northern Iowa won the MVC Tournament as a non-favorite with a modest 23-12 overall record and is making its first NCAA Tournament appearance since 2016, limiting their tournament-experience edge. Rick Pitino's Red Storm are battle-tested from a grueling Big East schedule and showed dominant late-season form with a 72-52 win over UConn, and Jay Bilas specifically cited Pitino's first-round coaching track record in picking St. John's.
